commit.message:
8216486: Rewrite jtreg test using the ClassFile API

Replace raw DataOutputStream bytecode generation with the ClassFile API
for readability. The class structure, methods, and bytecodes are now
expressed as readable Java code. A small patchBigMethodMaxes() utility
patches max_stack/max_locals after generation since the ClassFile API
auto-computes those values.

8216486: Extract datacount_overflow as a public static method

Move the overflow check from iterate_blocks into a standalone
BCEscapeAnalyzer::datacount_overflow() static method and use it
in iterate_blocks. This keeps the logic testable in isolation.

8216486: Add jtreg test for BCEscapeAnalyzer integer overflow

Generate a class with max_stack=65535, max_locals=65535, and 16384+
basic blocks so that (numblocks+1)*(max_stack+max_locals) overflows
a 32-bit int. On a UBSAN build the signed overflow is caught; on a
normal build the test verifies no crash from the resulting bogus
allocation size.

8379816: Revert accessor return types to int to avoid downstream mismatches

Keep field types as uint/_max_stack and u2/_max_locals/_num_blocks,
but return int from the accessors to prevent signed/unsigned comparison
warnings in 15+ call sites across opto, c1, and runtime code.
8379816: Use uint for max_stack and uint loop variables
8379816: Use unsigned return types for ci accessors and add braces

8379816: Use datacount64 naming for wide computation

Rename uint64_t variable to datacount64, then assign to size_t
datacount after the overflow check to avoid downstream casts.

8216486: Use unsigned types and add overflow check

Per review: use checked_cast<uint> for locals, uint64_t for datacount,
and bail out conservatively if allocation size overflows size_t.

8216486: Fix integer overflow in BCEscapeAnalyzer::iterate_blocks

Bail out conservatively if datacount overflows size_t.

8216486: Use int64_t instead of size_t for datacount

Per review feedback, use an explicit 64-bit type for the overflow fix.

8216486: Fix integer overflow in BCEscapeAnalyzer::iterate_blocks

Use size_t for datacount and datasize to prevent integer overflow
when numblocks, stkSize and numLocals are large. The product
(numblocks + 1) * (stkSize + numLocals) can exceed INT_MAX with
max classfile values (65535 each).
